sections.biography-source-undefined:
Yang Biao, styled Wenxian, was from Hongnong commandery. He was son of Yang Ci.

Like Yang Ci, Yang Biao would serve in prestigious positions. He was Minister over the Masses for a good part of the regime under Dong Zhuo before he was removed. Yang Biao opposed Dong Zhuo when the latter suggested relocating the capital to Chang’an from Luoyang. Huang Wan, a colleague of Yang Biao, was also removed for his defense of Yang Biao.

Yang Biao would live out his son, Yang Xiu, who was executed by Cao Cao. Yang Biao would also live out the Han and declined to serve in Wei despite a personal invitation by Cao Pi.

full_text:
Yang Biao (Wenxian) 楊彪 (文先)
ARomance of the Three KingdomsEncyclopedia officer profile.
Yang Biao (楊彪), styledWenxian (文先), was born in Hongnong. Stripped of rank and banished by both Dong Zhuo and Cao Cao. Drove Li Jue and Guo Si apart. Yang Biao was affiliated with the Han Dynasty, Dong Zhuo, Li Jue, and the Wei Kingdom. Yang Biao lived during the Later Han Dynasty and Three Kingdoms periods of Chinese history.

BiographySource Undefined
Yang Biao, styled Wenxian, was from Hongnong commandery. He was son of Yang Ci.
Like Yang Ci, Yang Biao would serve in prestigious positions. He was Minister over the Masses for a good part of the regime under Dong Zhuo before he was removed. Yang Biao opposed Dong Zhuo when the latter suggested relocating the capital to Chang’an from Luoyang. Huang Wan, a colleague of Yang Biao, was also removed for his defense of Yang Biao.
Yang Biao would live out his son, Yang Xiu, who was executed by Cao Cao. Yang Biao would also live out the Han and declined to serve in Wei despite a personal invitation by Cao Pi.
